item.title: Brazil finish strongly with 3-0 win over Scotland in Group C clash
item.description: Vinícius Júnior scored twice as Brazil sealed a comfortable 3-0 victory over Scotland in their final Group C match at the FIFA World Cup. Brazil took a 2-0 lead into half-time after goals from Vinícius and Matheus Cunha before Vinícius added his second in the second half to complete the victory.
item.content:encoded:
Vinícius Júnior scored twice as Brazil sealed a comfortable 3-0 victory over Scotland in their final Group C match at the FIFA World Cup.

Brazil took a 2-0 lead into half-time after goals from Vinícius and Matheus Cunha before Vinícius added his second in the second half to complete the victory.

Meanwhile, Switzerland defeated Canada 2-1 in Group B, while Morocco beat Haiti 4-2 in the other final Group C match.

Czechia will face Mexico, while South Africa meets South Korea at 1pm.

Coming up tomorrow, Curaçao takes on Côte d'Ivoire and Ecuador faces Germany at 8am. At 11am, Tunisia meets the Netherlands while Japan takes on Sweden. At 2pm, co-hosts the United States face Türkiye, while Australia takes on Paraguay.

item.title: Switzerland beat Canada 2-1 to top Group B in FIFA clash
item.description: Switzerland beat co-hosts Canada 2-1 to finish top of Group B and qualify for the FIFA World Cup knockout stage. The teams went into half-time level at 0-0.
item.content:encoded:
Switzerland beat co-hosts Canada 2-1 to finish top of Group B and qualify for the FIFA World Cup knockout stage.

The teams went into half-time level at 0-0.

Canada also advanced despite the defeat, reaching the knockout stage for the first time in their history after finishing second in the group.

Bosnia and Herzegovina defeated Qatar 3-1 in their final Group B match at the FIFA World Cup.

Brazil are currently taking on Scotland while Morocco is currently playing Haiti.

Czechia will play Mexico and South Africa meet South Korea at 1pm.

item.title: Fiji Women bracing for physical challenge against Queensland Reds
item.description: The Queensland Reds Women's rugby team boasts a powerful forward pack, led by players such as Eva Kapani and Tania Colony-Valley, which adds significant strength and impact to their ball-carrying game. This was shared to fijivillage Sports by McDonald Fiji Drua Women's Head Coach Mike Leggae.
item.content:encoded:
The Queensland Reds Women's rugby team boasts a powerful forward pack, led by players such as Eva Kapani and Tania Colony-Valley, which adds significant strength and impact to their ball-carrying game.

This was shared to fijivillage Sports by McDonald Fiji Drua Women's Head Coach Mike Leggae.

Leggae says they expect a similar approach to previous opponents, based on their video analysis, with perhaps a few variations.

He says overall, they anticipate a physical, direct style of play, much like what they have faced from other teams.

Legae says they had a good start and finish last weekend, but need to improve what happens in between.

He says for this weekend, it is all about achieving consistency.

The Fiji Drua Women will take on the Reds at 4.05pm Saturday

item.title: Ba Women aim to shine in OFC Women's Champions League
item.description: Ba Women will have the opportunity to test themselves against some of Oceania's top clubs when they compete in the OFC Women's Champions League in Honiara, Solomon Islands this weekend. Ba Football Association President Praneel Dayal says the team is well prepared and ready for the challenge ahead.
item.content:encoded:
Ba Women will have the opportunity to test themselves against some of Oceania's top clubs when they compete in the OFC Women's Champions League in Honiara, Solomon Islands this weekend.

Ba Football Association President Praneel Dayal says the team is well prepared and ready for the challenge ahead.

Dayal has urged the players to represent both Ba and Fiji with pride and determination throughout the tournament.

He says the competition is a special opportunity for the players to showcase their talent against some of the best clubs in Oceania.

Dayal says he has full confidence in the team and has encouraged the players to give their all in every match, stay united and make their supporters and the nation proud.

Ba will begin its Group B campaign against hosts Henderson Eels FC on Sunday at 5pm before taking on Papua New Guinea champions Hekari Women FC next Wednesday.

The Women in Black will then face Vanuatu's Tafea FC next Saturday in their final pool match.

The top two teams from each group will advance to the semi-finals, with the final scheduled for July 10th.

item.title: Moana Pasifika out of Super Rugby Pacific from 2027
item.description: Moana Pasifika will not take part in the 2027 Super Rugby Pacific competition after New Zealand Rugby confirmed it will not approve the transfer of the franchise’s licence to any new party. The decision follows the liquidation of the club and the withdrawal of its funders, the Pasifika Medical Association, despite efforts to keep the franchise in the competition.
item.content:encoded:
Moana Pasifika will not take part in the 2027 Super Rugby Pacific competition after New Zealand Rugby confirmed it will not approve the transfer of the franchise’s licence to any new party.

The decision follows the liquidation of the club and the withdrawal of its funders, the Pasifika Medical Association, despite efforts to keep the franchise in the competition.

In a statement, New Zealand Rugby says none of the bidders were able to satisfy both the capital and business plan requirements needed to secure the licence.

This means Super Rugby Pacific will move to a 10-team format in 2027.

New Zealand Rugby Chief Financial Officer Chris Kinraid says the decision was made in the best interests of the competition and to provide certainty and stability to sponsors, suppliers, other teams, broadcasters and fans.

Kinraid says the door remains open beyond 2027 if a sustainable long-term solution can be found for a team based in the Pacific Islands.

He says New Zealand Rugby remains willing to engage with any party that can meet the financial and commercial requirements needed to operate a competitive and sustainable team.

Kinraid also acknowledged the engagement of New Zealand Minister Winston Peters and the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Trade in recognising the role rugby can play in the Pacific region.

He says it has been a difficult period for Moana Pasifika players, management, fans and the communities that supported the team, and while a lot of work went into exploring all possible options for next season, the long-term financial requirements could not be met.

New Zealand Rugby says any party seeking to acquire the licence needed to demonstrate a credible long-term business plan capable of generating sustainable commercial revenue, as well as sufficient committed capital to meet establishment costs, support short-term operations and provide resilience against financial risks.

Kinraid says the assessment was that a party running a competitive team under the Moana Pasifika licence would need commercial revenue of more than NZ$10 million, in addition to broadcast revenue, and at least NZ$15 million in committed capital to support the business plan and maintain cash reserves.

He adds that New Zealand Rugby also received interest in potential future expansion opportunities beyond 2027, including in new markets and the Pacific, which it sees as an encouraging sign for the long-term strength and attractiveness of the competition.

Kinraid says the licence available through this process was specifically for a team representing Moana Pasifika as a pathway for Tonga and Samoa from 2027, and was not a process to establish a new team or enter a new market .

He says any future expansion of Super Rugby Pacific would need to be considered in partnership with Rugby Australia, Super Rugby Pacific and as part of a broader competition strategy.

Super Rugby Pacific is expected to confirm the format for the 2027 competition in the coming weeks.

Meanwhile, Kanaloa Consortium Chief Executive Tracy Atiga says they are disappointed they were not considered to take over the Moana Pasifika licence.

Atiga says the consortium will go through New Zealand Rugby’s statement and will respond in due course.

Kanaloa Consortium, which includes a number of former All Blacks of Pasifika heritage, had attempted to buy the Moana Pasifika licence in October last year after previously failing in a bid for a Super Rugby Pacific licence in 2021.

The consortium had also secured the support of the rugby unions of Tonga, Samoa, the Cook Islands and Niue.

Tonga Rugby Union President Lord Fakafanua, who is also the country’s Prime Minister, says it is a sad day to see Moana Pasifika come to an end after showing so much promise.

He says the union will issue a full response after discussing the matter further.

item.title: Crusaders captain David Havili heading to Japan
item.description: Crusaders captain David Havili is leaving New Zealand rugby for Japan.Havili played 30 Tests for New Zealand but has not featured
